finding.summary: Lead vocal track has an empty FX chain and is delivered at unity fader with true peak at 0.8 dBTP and sample peak at 0.2 dBFS, leaving essentially zero headroom for summing or inter-sample peaks. The 1/3-octave snapshot shows a warm, low-tilted balance (100 Hz at -18 dB, 1 kHz at -24 dB, 4 kHz at -30 dB), which is consistent with proximity effect and the absence of any high-pass or presence shaping. Dynamics are otherwise healthy (crest factor 12 dB, LRA 8 LU, integrated -20 LUFS, silence_fraction 0.05 so the measurement is not diluted by dead air).
finding.probable_cause: The vocal appears to have been tracked hot with the channel fader at unity and no inserts inserted — no HPF to manage proximity-driven low-end, no compressor/limiter to catch inter-sample peaks, no EQ to restore presence. With only three 1/3-octave bands reported, the warm tilt is suggestive but not high-confidence.
finding.confidence_reason: Isolation verified and silence_fraction is low (0.05), so level and spectrum readings are reliable. However, the spectrum is sparsely sampled (3 bands), the FX chain is empty so tonal moves are not directly actionable, and the mix bus context is unknown.

evidence_refs.description: 0.8 dBTP at unity fader; ~0 dB headroom
Channel fader at 0.0 dB, no attenuation
Empty FX chain; no HPF/EQ/limiter available to address peak or balance
1 kHz at -24 dB, 6 dB below 100 Hz
4 kHz at -30 dB, 12 dB below 100 Hz
12 dB — dynamics are healthy, no compression needed for control

proposal.reason: Preview changing track volume from 0.000 dB to -3.000 dB to test whether true_peak_db decreases.
